message:
refactor(ext4): use inode.mode==0 sentinel instead of bitmap gate

Swaps the inode-bitmap gate approach introduced in the previous
commit for a simpler sentinel: when delete_inode clears an inode
it now zeroes inode.mode as well as links_count / size / blocks,
and read_inode returns FfsError::NotFound when it parses an inode
whose mode is 0.

Why: reading the inode bitmap on every read_inode adds an extra
block read plus a metadata-csum verify for every inode
dereference. For filesystems with millions of inodes that
overhead is measurable, and the bitmap check is partially
redundant with the "inode has been zeroed" observation the on-
disk format already carries through dtime / mode / links_count.
Using mode == 0 as the single source-of-truth sentinel matches
what e2fsck uses when it scans for orphans and is what both
linux ext4 and e2fsprogs treat as the canonical "this inode is
unallocated" flag.

crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:

- read_inode drops the read_block_with_scope + verify_inode_
  bitmap_checksum + bitmap_get block. After parsing the inode
  from its inode-table bytes, it checks inode.mode == 0 and
  returns NotFound in that case, otherwise returns Ok(inode).
- The structured error message stays FfsError::NotFound(format!(
  \"inode {}\", ino.0)) so callers that match on the variant
  keep working.

crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs:

- delete_inode now also sets inode.mode = 0 alongside
  links_count / size / blocks. The corresponding unit test
  asserts mode is zero after deletion with the message
  "mode should be cleared".
